transport_findings.actual_transport: HTTPS XHR + fetch (mixed). Streaming reply for AI text NOT captured by window.fetch / XHR.prototype monkey-patches — strongly suggests bundled SDK uses fetch reference captured at module-init time before any user-land hook can intercept.

endpoint_observations.purpose: Fetch agent (서포터 / supporter character) configuration; returned len 466 sample below

endpoint_observations.transport: xmlhttprequest (per first observation in performance buffer)
endpoint_observations.purpose: User-message dispatch endpoint (sends prompt). Triggered exactly once per user prompt. NOT a long-lived stream — too short.
Polled — reads files attached to the conversation
Polled at end of stream — retrieves the COMPLETED assistant message after the streaming reply finishes. This is where Phase 6 could read the final text.

ai_replies_received: 오늘의 날씨 정보를 알려면 현재 위치가 필요하거나, 사용자가 원하시는 지역을 알려주시면 더욱 정확한 정보를 드릴 수 있습니다. 어느 지역의 날씨를 알고 싶으신가요?
서울은 대한민국의 수도이자 정치, 경제, 문화의 중심지인 매우 중요한 도시입니다. (...)
한국 음식 중에서는 김치, 비빔밥, 불고기, 잡채, 떡볶이 등이 특히 인기 있고, 저마다 고유한 맛과 특징이 있습니다. (...)

phase6_implications.socket_io_frame_injection_NOT_VIABLE: wrtn does not use Socket.IO at all on this build (web2/prod main-arm64-b1245225). Phase 6 frame manipulation strategy needs to be revised.
alternative_injection_strategies: DOM mutation: hook MutationObserver on chat scroll container, replace newly added '서포터' bubble text — this is what #964 already noted
fetch monkey-patch BEFORE wrtn bundle init: inject content-script at document_start that wraps fetch & reader.read() so chunked SSE-style chat-agent/messages response can be intercepted

intercept the GET /wrtn/v1/chats/{chatId} call (it's plain fetch, observed 70–80ms) — this returns the completed message JSON after stream end; mutating its response body would replace the rendered text
Capture from response of POST /wrtn/v1/chat-agent/messages — inspect Content-Type header to confirm SSE / NDJSON / chunked-JSON; then mirror that protocol when injecting
phase6_implications.next_step_phase4_recommendation: Re-capture by patching fetch from a content-script (document_start run_at) instead of console-injected hook; OR use chrome://inspect Network sniffing to read raw response bytes of the chat-agent/messages call which is the actual streaming endpoint.
